svg.text: Diagonal-Padded Expert Parallelism (4 experts on one 4090)
Idea: route contextualized samples by expert, pack into one block-diagonal batch, run grouped kernels, then scatter back.
1) Router bucketing
Token assignments (top-2 or top-1 FT):
E1: 512 seq chunks
E2: 476 seq chunks
E3: 603 seq chunks
E4: 409 seq chunks
Pad each bucket to tile multiple (e.g., 16 or 32)
Maintain per-expert attention masks
Keep context ids for scatter restore
Benefit: one launch path instead of 4 serial expert launches
2) Block-diagonal packed tensor
X_pack = diag(X_E1, X_E2, X_E3, X_E4)
E1 active block
E2 active block
E3 active block
E4 active block
3) Kernel execution
Run grouped GEMM / batched matmul on packed blocks
Y_pack = grouped_ffn(X_pack, W_E1..W_E4)
Single scheduling stream, fewer launch stalls
Higher occupancy than serial per-expert microbatches
Use fused scatter-gather where possible
Watchouts: pad waste, imbalance, routing jitter
Mitigation: cap max bucket ratio + entropy regularization
Fallback: split into 2 packed groups when one expert dominates
Restore token order via inverse index map.
Practical scheduler knobs for your 12-hour run
- pad_multiple: 16 for stability, 32 for throughput if VRAM allows
- max_tokens_per_pack: dynamic by free VRAM probe (leave 8-10% headroom)
- rebalance trigger: if largest_bucket / smallest_bucket > 1.8, force auxiliary load-balance pass
- context-preserving pad: pad with masked contextualized states, never raw zeros into attention path
- hourly KPI: tokens/sec, pad waste %, router entropy, contradiction-weighted loss
- expected gain vs serial experts on single GPU: typically 1.2x-1.6x (depends on routing skew)
Pseudo-flow:
route -> bucket_by_expert -> diagonal_pad_pack -> grouped_kernel -> scatter_restore -> loss
Keep tokenizer + SAT framing invariant across this whole loop.
File: docs/weekend_pipeline_05_diagonal_parallel.svg
